Notice Of Motion And Motion For Equitable Setoff Of Judgment And To Compel Satisfaction Of Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WALTER J. LIN, Eq. of Law Office of Michael Heath (Tel: 415-931-4207) for petitioner Hamdy Fayad;
Atty. SETH W. WIENER, Esq. of Law Offices of Seth W. Wiener (Tel: 925-487-5607) for defendants Gamal Amin and Nafisa Shafy.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER HAMDY FAYAD's Motion For Equitable Setoff Of Judgment And To Compel Satisfaction Of Judgment.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Hamdy Fayad's "motion for equitable setoff of judgment and to compel satisfaction of judgments" is denied.

Fayad has a 9/14/22 judgment against Gamal Amin and Nafisa Shafy. Four attorney-fee awards have since been entered against Fayad in this or other cases: 6/15/23 in favor of Shafy; 7/20/23 in favor of Silverman, LLC; 9/26/23 in favor of Silverman and 11/13/23 in favor of Machine Learning, Inc. Shafy, Shabab Amin and Gamal Amin. (Opp. 2:1-13.) Fayad wants all of the latter offset against the former.

"The key to setoff is the requirement of mutuality" - "the debts must be due to and from the same persons in the same capacity." (Prudential Reinsurance Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1118, 1127 [cleaned up].) But Silverman, Machine Learning and Shabab Amin are not parties to the 9/14/22 judgment. Moreover, it is best to address offset issues once, at litigation's end, rather than fight a time-consuming offset battle after each skirmish. (See Keith G. v. Suzanne H. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 853, 860.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-26-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Walter Lin, Esq. 415-931-4207 Law Offices of Michael Heath for Petitioner.
Seth Wiener, Esq. 925-487-5607 for OTHER SILVERMAN et al.
Arabic Interpreter H. Farag is present.

Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

OTHER SILVERMAN LLC'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs.

Silverman, LLC's motion for attorney fees and costs is granted in the amount of $6,361.25.
Plaintiff contracted to buy a corner store in San Francisco from defendants. The contract had an attorney fees provision, implicating Civil Code 1717. Plaintiff won an arbitration against defendants, then lost an attempt to amend his judgment to add Silverman on an alter ego theory. Silverman now seeks a section 1717 attorney fee award for opposing plaintiff's motion to amend.

Civil Code 1717 applies to "any action on a contract" with an attorney fees provision. (Id.) Included is any action that, as here, "involves" a contract. (In re Tobacco Cases I (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1591, 1601.) Moreover, section 1717 has been specifically applied to non-signatories like Silverman that defeat alter ego motions - on the theory that plaintiff would have been due 1717 attorney fees had he prevailed on alter ego. (See, e.g., MSY Trading Inc. v. Saleen Automotive, Inc. (2020) 51 Cal.App.5th 395.) The case our plaintiff relies on - Greenspan v. LADT LLC (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 486 - does not mention Civil Code 1717.

The $6,361.25 is reasonable; indeed, a fee award this low is unusual.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
